content_of_post_to_answer: I am having a problem. So I am asking this question. I have so many problems, like procrastination, overthinking, anger issues, etc. Should I set a yearly goal like that - "I am a person who controls procrastination", like that?
answer: I wouldn’t make a yearly goal like “I am a person who controls procrastination.” That bundles several different problems into an identity. Procrastination, overthinking, and anger aren’t one enemy; they’re often different responses to different situations. A more useful question is: what do I do when each one shows up? Identities are hard to measure. Behaviors are visible. People usually change faster when they stop trying to become a different person and start interrupting specific patterns.
